Title: Uncovering a miltiradiene biosynthetic gene cluster in the Lamiaceae reveals a dynamic evolutionary trajectory

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: terpene synthase, class II diterpene synthase, TPS-c
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: abietane diterpenoid / miltiradiene-related diterpene backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to (+)-CPP as the class II diterpene precursor for downstream diterpene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized diterpenes in Lamiaceae are associated with ecological interactions and defense; (+)-kaurene-derived calliterpenone has plant growth-promoting potential.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root; fruit; flower; young leaf depending on pathway module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Second half of the C. americana BGC; more strongly expressed in flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, preferentially in flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with coexpression of PbDXS and PbGGPPS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS product profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides (+)-CPP precursor used with CamTPS10 to form (+)-kaurene; supports a distinct diterpene metabolic module in C. americana.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from 8 C. americana tissues (SRA accessions not specified individually in text)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamTPS7 was experimentally confirmed as a (+)-CPP synthase. 2. Its expression pattern groups with the second half of the C. americana BGC, suggesting a distinct metabolic module. 3. It provides precursor for CamTPS10-dependent (+)-kaurene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing (+)-CPP-dependent diterpene pathways and for engineering access to (+)-kaurene/calliterpenone-related scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CamTPS7 is one of two (+)-CPP synthases in the C. americana cluster and supports BGC bifunctionality.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: terpene synthase, class I diterpene synthase, TPS-e.2.1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (+)-CPP to miltiradiene; abietatriene can also appear via spontaneous aromatization in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Miltiradiene is a precursor to abietane diterpenoids with antimicrobial, antioxidant, anticancer and anti-inflammatory activities.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: First half of the C. americana BGC; preferentially expressed in fruit and root tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, enriched in fruit and root tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; root metabolite extraction from C. americana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS comparison with published miltiradiene synthase products; root metabolite validation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces miltiradiene, establishing one core metabolic module of the C. americana BGC.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from 8 C. americana tissues; metabolite profiling by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamTPS9 was confirmed as a miltiradiene synthase. 2. Together with CamTPS6 it defines a root/fruit-expressed module of the BGC. 3. Its product serves as substrate for multiple cluster CYP76AH oxidations.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value entry enzyme for synthetic production of abietane diterpenoid scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Abietatriene observed likely results from spontaneous aromatization of miltiradiene in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: terpene synthase, class I diterpene synthase, TPS-e
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: (+)-kaurene-derived diterpene biosynthesis within the C. americana BGC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (+)-CPP to (+)-kaurene but not ent-CPP to ent-kaurene.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Calliterpenone, a (+)-kaurene-derived diterpenoid, has plant growth-promoting potential.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Second half of the C. americana BGC; strongly expressed in flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, enriched in flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with CamTPS7 or other CPP synthases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR for previously unpublished activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates (+)-kaurene, revealing a second diterpene backbone module and bifunctionality of the BGC.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from 8 C. americana tissues; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamTPS10 was identified as a (+)-kaurene synthase. 2. It uses (+)-CPP rather than ent-CPP. 3. Its recruitment into the cluster supports evolution of a second metabolic branch in C. americana.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables engineering access to (+)-kaurene/calliterpenone scaffolds and non-natural diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors highlight this as a rare example of BGC bifunctionality in plants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes miltiradiene and abietatriene to multiple oxygenated abietane diterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: oxidized abietane diterpenoids derived from miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abietane diterpenoids in Lamiaceae often have antimicrobial and medicinal activities.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: First half of the C. americana BGC; associated with root/fruit module.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, module associated with fruit and root expression.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with diterpene synthases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; purification and NMR of one product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts miltiradiene/abietatriene into several oxidized abietane products, functionally validating the oxidative capacity of the BGC.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamCYP76AH67 oxidized miltiradiene-derived backbones to six products. 2. One purified product was assigned as 15-hydroxy-ent-abieta-8,11,13-triene. 3. The enzyme supports formation of oxidized abietane diterpenoids from the BGC miltiradiene module.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product mixture indicates catalytic promiscuity, which may complicate selective engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful oxidative tailoring enzyme for generating diverse abietane diterpenoids in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically divergent from many other BGC CYP76AHs and closer to CYP76AK-related clade.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: abietatriene-enriched profile and oxidized abietatriene product (2c)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts on miltiradiene and likely hydroxylates the c-ring, facilitating conversion toward abietatriene and yielding an oxidized abietatriene product.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abietane diterpenoids are associated with plant defense and multiple human health-related bioactivities.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: First half of the C. americana BGC; associated with root/fruit module.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, module associated with fruit and root expression.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; C. americana root extract analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; comparison with root extract metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of abietatriene and a further oxidized abietatriene product, supporting enzymatic assistance in miltiradiene aromatization/oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamCYP76AH68 showed activity with miltiradiene. 2. It strongly shifted product profile toward abietatriene and produced oxidized abietatriene 2c. 3. Matching compounds were detected in C. americana root extract, supporting in planta relevance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic route is inferred and product structure not fully resolved for all compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme to improve flux from miltiradiene toward oxidized abietane products in synthetic biology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose this enzyme may assist the otherwise spontaneous conversion of miltiradiene to abietatriene.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: (+)-copalol / manool oxidation branch associated with the second BGC module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of (+)-manool from (+)-copalol, the dephosphorylation product of (+)-CPP.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Second half of the C. americana BGC; associated with flower and young leaf expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, module associated with flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms (+)-manool from (+)-copalol, establishing activity of the CYP71D-containing module.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamCYP71D717 unexpectedly did not oxidize (+)-kaurene. 2. Instead, it catalyzed production of (+)-manool from (+)-copalol. 3. This defines chemistry associated with the second C. americana BGC module.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Off-pathway use of dephosphorylated precursor may reduce desired flux toward other diterpene end products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to access manool-type diterpenes in heterologous production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity was discovered when testing CYPs against possible diterpene intermediates; indicates module chemistry differs from initial expectation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: (+)-manool oxidation branch associated with the second BGC module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Second half of the C. americana BGC; associated with flower and young leaf expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. americana, module associated with flower and young leaf tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR confirmation of product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts (+)-manool to 3(S)-hydroxy-(+)-manool when combined with CamCYP71D717.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamCYP71D716 acted downstream of CamCYP71D717. 2. Together they converted (+)-copalol-derived (+)-manool fully to 3(S)-hydroxy-(+)-manool. 3. This validates oxidative tailoring activity in the second BGC module.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for stepwise engineering of oxygenated manool diterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The hydroxylated position corresponds to the position of a keto group in calliterpenone-derived chemistry discussed by the authors.
